text:
In the stillness of night, the wind takes flight,  
A howling whisper, a haunting delight.  
It rattles the windows, a ghostly refrain,  
While shadows dance softly, invoking the rain.  
Wrapped in warm blankets, I listen and sigh,  
The world fades away, just the wind and I.

This chalk stream is much altered by man, shackled by embankments on both sides and overly straightened, deepened and widened. It doesn't operate quite as it should, but still offers refuge for a host of wildlife (and evocative scenes like this).

#rivers #chalkstreams

Swedish scientists have developed a living computer* using human brain tissue.  

It consists of 16 lab-grown organoids that send and receive information like a traditional computer chip but use far less energy—just 10 to 20 watts compared to 21 megawatts

text:
Medical specialties are named logically

Nephros = kidney, -ology = study of
Cardiology: kardia = heart

But then you come to obstetrics and orthopaedics

Obstetric = the one who stands opposite (to catch a projectile neonate)

Orthopaedic = straight child (eg fixing club feet/scoliosis etc)

Okay - I’ll take up this challenge.

text:
What’s the most annoying, fake “climate factoid” you see peddled by influencers on the internet?

Mine? That ocean plants “produce 70% of the world’s oxygen.”

That’s incorrect. Terrestrial plants generate about 55% of our planet’s oxygen. Marine ecosystems produce about 45%.

text:

text:
A while back I went to visit Prodigy Minigolf in Easthampton, MA.  An amazing video game collection - you can  sign out a game from their huge library and play well known stuff as well as obscure titles.  Retrogaming paradise.

Can you think of anything they don't have?

text:
I started to notice the jig when Drake released “Nice For What”. 

He was rap-singing a love letter to the women he has historically ignored and/or berated.

That’s when my brain started to wonder if it was Oochie Wally or One Mic.
